title: Summary Programmable Logic Controllers
content: University, conducted a hands-on workshop on Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs). The workshop, attended by 21 students, aimed to provide an in-depth understanding of PLC systems and their role in industrial automation. The session covered the basics of PLC architecture, programming fundamentals, and real-world applications in industries such as manufacturing, robotics, and process control. The participants gained valuable experience by working on PLC simulators, designing and implementing basic programs, which helped bridge the gap between theory and practical knowledge. The event featured insightful sessions led by Dr. Meenakshi Awasthi and Dr. Aditya Nath Bhatt, who discussed the significance of PLCs in modern automation. Participants showcased their skills through practical tasks, which were evaluated by expert mentors. Exceptional performers were recognized in a concluding ceremony. The workshop emphasized interactive learning, with support from lab technicians, Mr. Ajeet Kumar, Mr. Narendra Kumar, Mr. Arun Kumar, Mr. Deep Chandra, and Mr. Mahendra Singh, ensuring a smooth and engaging experience for all attendees.
